title: teucrium eriocephalum subsp. lutescens
intro: Teucrium eriocephalum subsp. lutescens, commonly known as the yellow-headed germander, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of anxiety and stress, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. The yellow-headed germander can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate respiratory issues.
